candidate_mechanism: shrink-fit lashed-adapter module composition where adapter tension increases under task/adversarial load, enabling flexible impact-tolerant agent composition with local repair

results.judge_reason: Source-domain qajaq description; no LLM/adapter/module-composition content, so functional equivalence to the proposed ML mechanism is essentially zero.
Pure source-side article on sealskin-over-frame kayaks; nothing about ML modules or adapters.
Source-domain encyclopedia entry mentioning shrink-fit rawhide; same biological/cultural object the candidate draws from, no LLM bridge.
Adapter-based adversarial training is related but does not propose tension-under-load lashing tightening or shrink-fit interface tensioning at module boundaries.
Smallest weight interventions for unlearning/robustness is conceptually distant from shrink-fit lashing adapters that tighten under impact load.
Cited in novelty claim as decomposing models into modules but lacking the shrink-fit tension-under-load mechanism, so it does not preempt the proposal.
Brick-style configurable foundation models share modular composition framing but not the impact-tightening lashing mechanism.
Modular toolbox for jailbreak research is methodological infrastructure, not a load-tensioning adapter composition mechanism.

notes: Source-side qajaq pages describe the physical object the candidate borrows from, which is expected and not a prior-art collision. LLM-side adapter/modular-model papers cover adjacent concepts but none implement the shrink-fit, tension-rises-under-impact lashing mechanism the candidate proposes.
